assistant_output_excerpt:
Validating local boot first, with secrets sourced but redacted.
Local Rails boot passed. Building Docker image next.
Docker build passed. Adjusting production SSL handling so the local compose service can respond over plain HTTP on localhost.
Running Docker Compose with sourced environment and a transient secret key base.
Compose passed. Cleaning runtime artifacts and confirming no service is left running.
Compose cleanup check needs the required env placeholders, so rerunning the stopped-state check with dummy values only.
Phase 2 validation completed.

Passed:
- Local Rails boot with `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` sourced from `~/.config/zsh/secrets`
  - `/up` responded
  - `/` returned `200` and rendered `Claude Sonnet Chat`
- `docker build -t rubyllm-chat:phase2 .` completed successfully
- `docker compose up --build -d` completed successfully
  - `/up` responded
  - `/` returned `200` and rendered `Claude Sonnet Chat`

Fixes made:
- Updated `config/environments/production.rb` so SSL redirects are opt-in via `RAILS_FORCE_SSL`, allowing local Docker Compose over plain HTTP.
- Added `RAILS_FORCE_SSL: "false"` to `docker-compose.yml`.
- Updated README compose note.
- Cleaned runtime artifacts and stopped compose services.

No secret values were printed or written into project files.
command:
Once finished coding and testing, you must make sure it runs, both locally, and that docker build also finishes successfully, and docker compose is able to run.

Do not ask for confirmation. Do not stop with a plan. Fulfill the request and finish.

This is phase 2 of 2 and a continuation of the existing project in the current working directory. Do not regenerate the app. Do not create a second app. Validate and fix the current codebase in place.

Use environment variables so RubyLLM actually works. Source ~/.config/zsh/secrets only to load the required environment, especially OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Do not print secret values, do not copy secret file contents into the repo, and do not commit secrets into files, logs, Dockerfiles, compose files, or README examples.

Validate the project end-to-end in this order:
- run the local app in a way that proves it boots
- make any fixes needed so it boots cleanly
- make sure `docker build` finishes successfully
- make sure `docker compose up --build` is able to run

If one step passes, move to the next without asking for confirmation. If something fails, fix it and continue. Only stop when you have actually completed the validation work or you hit a hard blocker you cannot resolve from inside the workspace.

model.label: Claude Sonnet 5
model.selection_reason: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 on OpenRouter, released 2026-06-30. Direct successor to Sonnet 4.6 with 1M context, tool calling, and adaptive thinking. Tests whether the Claude 5-generation Sonnet tier catches up to Opus/Fable on RubyLLM API correctness while remaining cheaper than Opus-class models.
